finding_results.notes: Rebuilt Thessa's condemnation around the Crook-specific technical finding: Maren blew contamination past standard catch lines and created false-clean centers, rather than repeating the style-bible calibration phrasing.
The arrival now states why the assessment waits for dawn and shows the concrete overnight safeguard: Denna's marked sleep line, outer-shelter placement, and Ruald/Kael watch rotation.

finding_results.notes: The Crook roster now introduces Petra, Kael, Ruald, Denna, and Hale with distinct identifiers. Hale is framed as outside the working registry, which preserves the continuity-sheet count of five active sin-eaters while still introducing the full six-person camp present for the downstream casualty accounting. Root-chewing now belongs to Ruald rather than Kael.

The opening tension now turns on containment procedure and operational assessment instead of replaying a public fraud exposure. Thessa explicitly refuses to rerun Silt in the dark and shifts the scene toward dawn testing.
Kept a single stained-hands reference on Thessa's reintroduction, removed the later stained-hands repeats, and cut the 'thirty years' phrasing from the bowl description.

Revised all three cited diagnostic-speech blocks. Block 1 (inverted-pattern diagnosis, lines 159-173): Kael now interrupts Thessa mid-sentence with his own competing read from the scan, supplies practitioner shorthand ('Like pouring through a cracked bowl'), and completes part of the explanation ('Because he's shaved the top off it') before Thessa finishes. Removed narrator commentary about Thessa's voice hardening into 'something instructional.' Block 2 (consumption argument, lines 191-217): Petra interrupts Thessa's case mid-sentence with her own reading ('We can read that much'), breaking the single long justification block into competing practitioner voices. Kael's consumption-capacity assessment broken with a physical beat. Thessa's outbreak given more contraction and a self-correction false start ('don't — they don't work'). Dropped narrator tag 'Each word came clipped and even now.' Block 3 (post-test assessment, lines 303-349): Denna now supplies the outer-marker confirmation, breaking Kael's opening report. Kael completes Thessa's mechanistic explanation ('Carried the drop instead of the spill') rather than Thessa delivering it as a monologue. Historical reference compressed. Kael interrupts Thessa's 'sloppy' assessment with his own observation about the channel tear. Training terms split between circle-address and direct-to-Maren address. No speaker delivers more than two consecutive clean diagnostic sentences without another practitioner redirecting the beat in any of the three revised blocks.

pass_summaries.summary: Reframed the Crook arrival around professional containment procedure, introduced the full downstream roster with Hale outside the active registry, rewrote Thessa's core accusation in chapter-specific contamination language, and removed the repeated Thessa descriptor pattern while preserving the chapter's must-land beats and ending state.

Single finding addressed across three passage blocks. Broke orderly question-to-speech diagnostic ladders in the examination scene, consumption argument, and post-test assessment by distributing information across competing practitioner voices. Kael now actively co-reads and interrupts rather than feeding Thessa cues; Petra and Denna supply their own observations rather than teeing up explanations. Thessa retains authority and all operational information remains legible, but the exchanges now arrive under practitioner pressure with interruptions, partial diagnoses, and physical-beat redirections. Added contraction and self-correction texture to Thessa's emotional outbreak. Removed narrator commentary that explained how dialogue sounded. All must-land beats preserved: the Crook arrival, examination findings, consumption argument, supervised test, 'not entirely useless' acknowledgment, and training terms.
